title: Jedi Initiate
content:
{{Top}} {{Youmay|the group of [[Jedi]] children|the [[Nightsisters|Nightsister]] [[Initiate|rank]]}} {{Update|[[The High Republic Adventures (2022) 5]], [[The High Republic: Cataclysm]], [[What a Jedi Makes]], [[The High Republic: Out of the Shadows]], [[The High Republic: Midnight Horizon]], [[The High Republic: Trail of Shadows 5]], [[Dooku: Jedi Lost]], [[Inquisitor: Rise of the Red Blade]], [[Queen's Hope]], [[Brotherhood (novel)|Brotherhood]], [[A Jedi's Duty]], [[Reign of the Empire: The Mask of Fear]], [[Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order]], [[Part V]], [[Star Wars Jedi: Survivor]], [[Yoda 4]], [[Yoda 5]], [[Masters]], [[Shadow of the Sith]], {{HelmetCollectionCite|72|Weapons & Uniforms}}, {{EncyclopediaCite|Lightsabers and Jedi Equipment}}}} {{TitleOrPosition |image=[[File:Youngling.svg]] |name=Jedi Initiate |establishedby=[[Jedi Order]]{{Databank|jedi-youngling|Jedi Youngling}} |dateestablished= |datesuspended=*[[19 BBY]]The fall of the [[Jedi Order]] occurred during the events of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], which ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates to be during [[19 BBY]]. Therefore, the [[Jedi]] Order fell in 19 BBY and the title of Jedi Initiate was suspended in 19 BBY as well. *[[28 ABY]]According to ''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'', the [[Jedi Temple of Luke Skywalker]] was destroyed 6 [[Standard year|years]] before the "[[Hosnian Cataclysm|Starkiller Incident]]." Since ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates the Starkiller Incident to [[34 ABY]], the destruction must take place in 28 ABY. Therefore, it can be deduced that the Jedi Initiates was suspended in 28 ABY as well. |datereestablished=Between [[15 ABY]]According to ''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'', [[Luke Skywalker]] began rebuilding the [[Jedi]] Order with [[Ben Solo]] as his first student 19 [[Standard year|years]] before the [[Hosnian Cataclysm]]. Since ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates the Hosnian Cataclysm to [[34 ABY]], the new generation of Jedi began in [[15 ABY]]. and [[21 ABY]]''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'' places the events of ''Shadow of the Sith'', which saw [[Jedi Initiate]]s being trained at the [[Jedi Temple of Luke Skywalker]] thirteen [[Standard year|years]] before the [[Hosnian Cataclysm|Starkiller Incident]], which ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates to [[34 ABY]]. Therefore, ''Shadow of the Sith'' takes place in [[21 ABY]] and that the Jedi Initiates were restored by [[21 ABY]]. |dateabolished= |government=[[Jedi High Council]]{{Databank|adi-gallia|Adi Gallia}} |organization=[[Jedi]] Order |type=Jedi rank |term= |powers= |duties= }} {{Quote|I'm only an Initiate. A Master hasn't even chosen me as a Padawan yet.|[[Reina Bilass]], to [[Lohim Nara]]|{{StoryCite|story=What a Jedi Makes|book=Stories of Jedi and Sith}}}} '''Jedi Initiates''', also known as '''Jedi younglings''', were [[Force-sensitive]] [[Youngling|children]] recruited by the [[Jedi Order]] from various [[species]] across [[the galaxy]]. At an early age they began their initial lessons in the [[Jedi training|Jedi arts]] at the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant]], although their training also extended to the [[planet]] [[Ilum]] where younglings were required to undergo a sacred rite of passage known as [[Gathering|the Gathering]] where the younglings would claim their [[kyber crystal]] to use in their [[lightsaber]]. Before the [[time]] came for a youngling to advance to a higher degree of education, [[Grand Master]] [[Yoda]] served as an instructor in the ways of [[the Force]] to all younglings in the course of his long career. In order to qualify for the rank of [[Padawan]], younglings had to complete a series of tests—the [[Initiate Trials]]—in order to become apprenticed to more experienced [[Jedi]] such as [[Jedi Knight|Knights]] and [[Jedi Master|Masters]]. Over the course of the [[Clone Wars]], the Jedi Order's future was threatened by machinations of [[Darth Sidious]] the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] and nemesis of the Jedi. Seeking to train a force of [[Dark side of the Force|dark side]] acolytes who would serve as his enforcers in the [[New Order]], Sidious orchestrated the abduction of several Force-sensitive infants who were chosen to become Jedi younglings. The Jedi succeeded in foiling his plot, however. Yet despite their efforts to safeguard the next generation of Jedi, the younglings perished along with the majority of the Jedi Order as a result of [[Order 66]] and the subsequent [[Proclamation of the New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire]]. ==Overview== ===Future of the Jedi=== {{Quote|What's on the crystal?''"
"''A list of every known Force-sensitive child in the galaxy. The future Jedi… The future of the Jedi Order.|Ahsoka Tano and Mace Windu|{{TCW|Holocron Heist}}}} [[File:PloDiscoversAhsoka.png|thumb|left|300px|Force-sensitive children represented the future of the Jedi Order.]] The [[Jedi Order]], a monastic organization dedicated to the preservation of peace and justice in the [[Galactic Republic]],{{Databank|jedi-order|Jedi Order}} recruited its members very young, but not typically straight after birth, as [[Wee Dunn]] was considered too young to be taken in by the Order as an infant.{{TCW|Children of the Force}} Utilizing [[DNA|blood]] tests to estimate the [[midi-chlorian]] count of prospective students,{{Film|I}} the Order was able to identify [[Force-sensitive]] [[youngling]]s of various [[species]] throughout [[the galaxy]]. Before their training, younglings were frequently only skilled in a single aspect of the Force, while they would begin to advance in others once their training began.[[Ahsoka (novel)|''Ahsoka'']] After their sensitivity to [[the Force]] was confirmed, the infants or more usually young children{{TCW|The Gathering (episode)}} were brought to the [[Jedi crèche]] of the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant]], where they were to live until they were chosen to become a [[Padawan]].{{Film|II}} Some of these younglings were rescued from dangerous situations such as [[Dooku]], whose father left him to die after learning of his infant son's Force-sensitivity and [[Depa Billaba]] and her sister [[Sar Labooda]] whose parents were killed by pirates.''[[Dooku: Jedi Lost]]'' ''[[Star Wars Character Encyclopedia: Updated and Expanded]]'' Initiates were usually brought to the Temple very young. However, old arrivals included the nine-year old [[Anakin Skywalker]], the five-year-old [[Rael Averross]],''[[Master & Apprentice]]'' and the eight-year-old [[Verosha Aniseya]], who went on to leave the Order.{{Acolyte|Lost / Found}} Upon entering the Order, they quickly learned that their true family was the [[Jedi]] Order. [[Marda Ro]] of the [[Path of the Closed Fist]] accused the Jedi Order of cruelty by taking children away from their families,''[[The High Republic: Path of Vengeance]]'' but the Jedi actively sought the permission of families when finding younglings.{{Acolyte|Destiny (The Acolyte)}} The Jedi maintained a list pertaining to confirmed Force-sensitive infants, particularly their identities and locations.{{TCW|Cargo of Doom}} Due to the children's importance to the future of the Jedi, the [[Jedi High Council|High Council]] went to great lengths to keep the list's contents internal to the Order. The list itself was contained within the [[kyber memory crystal]] which could only be accessed through a [[holocron]], an informational device stored within the [[Holocron Vault|vaults]] of the Temple. Only a Force-sensitive, trained in the use of [[telekinesis]], had the ability to combine the [[crystal]] with a holocron. The crystal itself was entrusted to a member of the Order who served as its keeper.{{Databank|bolla-ropal|Bolla Ropal}} ===Youngling clans=== {{Quote|Younglings… ''Younglings''. A visitor, we have.|Yoda, introducing [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] to the Bear Clan|}} Jedi younglings were divided into ten clans consisting of approximately twenty students. Among these groups was [[Bear Clan]], a class of younglings between the ages of four and eight. The [[Clan (Jedi)|youngling clans]] would remain as one unit, living and training together, until the [[time]] came to go their separate ways as Padawan learners.[[Kanan 7|''Kanan'' 7]] The younglings who were not chosen to become Padawans were supervised by the [[Council of Reassignment]].''[[Star Wars Super Graphic: A Visual Guide to a Galaxy Far, Far Away]]'' ===Instruction=== {{Quote|Reach out. Sense the Force around you. Use your feelings, you must.|Yoda's instructions, to the members of Bear Clan|}} [[File:JediYoungling.png|thumb|right|300px|The new generation of Jedi Knights began their training as younglings under the guidance of Grand Master Yoda.]] Upon joining a clan, younglings began their basic training in the Jedi arts. Their lessons included various tests and rituals that had to be completed in order to advance to the higher levels of the Jedi arts. Between the ages of four and eight,{{Databank|bear-clan|Bear Clan}} younglings acquired the basic skills of [[lightsaber combat]] using low-powered{{SWE|groups|jediorder|Jedi Order}} [[Training lightsaber|training sabers]] to practice the deflection of [[blaster]] shots fired by training [[droid]]s. Both the training droids and lightsabers were non-lethal.''[[The High Republic: Convergence]]'' The next step in their progress as aspiring Jedi was the construction of a genuine [[lightsaber]]—the signature weapon of the Jedi which first required a [[kyber crystal]]. This lesson ultimately led to the creation of [[Gathering|the Gathering]]; an ancient and significant tradition that began [[Standard century|centuries]] before the events of the Clone Wars,{{Databank|ilum|Ilum}} the Gathering tested the younglings' ability to locate their particular crystal through the Force. It required younglings to travel offworld from the Jedi Temple on Coruscant to the [[Crystal Cave]] of [[Ilum]], a frigid world{{SWE|locations|ilum|Ilum}} located in the [[Unknown Regions]]''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]'' and most sacred to the Jedi Order. After acquiring their crystal, the younglings had to use the Force to telekinetically combine all of the necessary components into a lightsaber.{{TCW|A Necessary Bond}} For almost a [[millennium]] of the Jedi Order's history,{{Film|V}} all younglings were trained by [[Yoda]],{{TCW|Voices}} the ancient [[Grand Master]]{{Databank|yoda|Yoda}} who also served as an instructor in the Jedi arts for eight centuries by the time of the Clone Wars. Yoda provided the younglings with a beginner's education in lightsaber combat, a practice which incorporated lessons on using the Force to guide and enhance their reflexes. In addition to teaching the basic skills of lightsaber combat, Yoda also served as a guide for younglings who participated in the Gathering. [[File:The_Gathering_TG.png|thumb|left|300px|In addition to lightsaber training, Yoda served as a guide for younglings who undertook the Gathering.]] [[Tera Sinube]], another ancient Jedi Master, also took on the responsibility of overseeing the younglings' development as [[lightsaber duel]]ists.{{TCW|The Wrong Jedi}} In addition to guiding the development of their students, it was the Master's prerogative to introduce a Padawan as a guest lecturer to a youngling clan.{{TCW|Lightsaber Lost}} The task of educating younglings in the proper construction of a lightsaber was the duty of [[Huyang]]; an [[architect droid]] [[professor]] based out on the Jedi [[Starship|vessel]], the ''[[Crucible]]'', he personally oversaw the creation of lightsabers by younglings of a thousand generations—a record which included the one-time youngling Yoda.{{TCW|A Test of Strength}} ==History== ===High Republic Era=== {{Dialogue|Herald Werth Plouth|The Jedi came for you when you were young. To train you. To teach you how to bind the Force to your will.|Guide Marda Ro|But they didn't take her. They took her sister instead, leaving her behind… because she wasn't good enough.|"The Mother" Elecia Zeveron|You don't know what you're talking about. ''[…]'' I didn't love my sister. She was nothing. They got it wrong. They should've taken me, not her.|Guide Marda Ro|And now you're making them pay. You're making ''everyone'' pay. For their mistake. For not taking you seriously.|attr=[[Herald (title)|Herald]] [[Werth Plouth]], "the Mother" [[Elecia Zeveron]], and [[Guide of the Closed Fist|Guide]] [[Marda Ro]] uncover the Mother's past|src=}} [[File:Yoda5-Textless.png|thumb|right|300px|Jedi Master Yoda oversaw the training of all Jedi younglings.]] Grand Master Yoda trained all Jedi as younglings. During the [[High Republic Era]], youngling [[Gella Nattai]] was a quiet girl but a curious mind, trying to find passages or tombs throughout the Jedi Temple and wandering its halls. She assumed she would be a natural at lightsaber combat and was the first one to arrive at lightsaber training, where she was to wield a training lightsaber against [[remote]]s. Unaware that the training spheres were non-lethal, she became scared of its many [[photoreceptor]]s and ran from the room when she saw one, but she was found by Yoda in a temple garden. Together, they watched a frog repeatedly attempt to leap over a stone wall to reach a waterfall. Taught by Yoda to overcome fear and always try again, like that frog, she arrived at training the next day and was hit on her nose by a training blast. However, she overcame her fear as she continued to practice. One day during the High Republic Era, a [[Unidentified Jedi seeker|Jedi seeker]] approached the [[Zeveron family]], who had two Force-sensitive daughters; [[Elecia Zeveron]] had mild Force-sensitivity''[[Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia]]'' that gave her a natural skill in [[mind trick]]s, while the more Force-sensitive [[Oliviah Zeveron]] held skill in [[telekinesis]]. The seeker ultimately took Oliviah to be trained as a Jedi, while Elecia's abilities were seen as too weak. Unfortunately for the Order, that decision would spell a lifetime of rage for Elecia. Believing she should have been chosen instead, her anger at the Jedi for being overlooked led her down a path that saw her try to take over the galaxy as "the [[Mother (title)|Mother]]" of the [[Path of the Open Hand]]. During the [[Battle of Jedha]] instigated by the Mother''[[The High Republic: Cataclysm]]'' in [[382 BBY]], Oliviah spotted Elecia and unearthed memories of her past, which she confronted Elecia with during the [[Night of Sorrow]]. ===Late Republic Era=== During the [[Clone Wars]], the [[Sith]] arranged for the [[Raid on the Jedi Temple|theft]] of both a holocron and the memory crystal in order to abduct members of the Jedi's next generation. [[Darth Sidious]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] publicly known as [[Supreme Chancellor]] Sheev Palpatine,{{Databank|emperor-palpatine-darth-sidious|Emperor Palpatine/Darth Sidious}} intended to use the younglings in an experimental procedure to create a force of [[Dark side of the Force|dark side]] adepts that would serve him in the [[New Order]]. However, the plan was thwarted by the efforts of the Jedi, particularly the [[Jedi Knight]] Anakin Skywalker and his Padawan, [[Ahsoka Tano]], both of whom rescued the children from captivity and returned them to their parents. [[File:UnidentifiedClan-RotS.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Consumed by the persona of Darth Vader, the former Anakin Skywalker murdered a group of Jedi younglings.]] Later, Skywalker became instrumental in the fall of the Order following his seduction to the dark side as the [[Sith Lord]] [[Darth]] Vader. With the initiation of [[Order 66]], Vader led a [[Republic Military|military]] [[Operation: Knightfall|assault]] on the Temple at the behest of his new [[Sith Master|Master]], who commanded him to spare none of his former comrades. This command extended to the younglings, a group of which were confronted in the [[Jedi Council Chamber|High Council Chamber]] by Vader. [[Unidentified Jedi youngling|One boy]], recognizing Skywalker and thinking he was still a Jedi Knight, asked him what they should do against the overwhelming number of clones, but Vader did not respond and instead slaughtered the children. In the aftermath of the [[Galactic Empire]]'s [[Proclamation of the New Order|rise to power]], the majority of Jedi were slain along with the youngest of their brethren, resulting in the [[Great Jedi Purge|destruction]] of the next generation of the Jedi Order.{{Film|III}} ===Legacy and further efforts=== {{Quote|In the old days, Force-sensitive children from across the galaxy were selected to begin the first steps on the path to becoming a Jedi. These young initiates lived in clans at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and were trained in the Jedi arts by legendary masters.|Luke Skywalker reflects on Jedi history|''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Jedi]]''}} Despite the loss of the Order, the remnants of the Jedi endured throughout the [[Imperial Era|Dark Times]], surviving both the Sith and their Empire.{{Film|VI}} However, the survivors elected to remain secret, so many Force-sensitive children were left in the galaxy without the chance of becoming Jedi. When the Empire located any Force-sensitive children, they were taken by Vader[[Darth Vader (2017) 19|''Darth Vader'' (2017) 19]] or members of the [[Inquisitorius]] to be made part of [[Project Harvester]], fulfilling Sidious's plan from the Clone Wars.{{Rebels|The Future of the Force}} Two surviving Jedi, Master [[Vanzell Mar-Klar]] and Padawan [[Nedriss Narr]], attempted to train younglings away from Imperial eyes.''[[Jedi Training: Trials of the Temple]]'' After the fall of the Empire, the Jedi Order was reborn as a new, albeit short-lived, generation under [[Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker]].{{Film|VII}} The Jedi practice of recruiting younglings also served as inspiration to [[Commandant]] [[Brendol Hux]], an [[Imperial Academy]] instructor who greatly admired the training methods of both the Jedi Order and [[Grand Army of the Republic|Grand Army]] [[clone trooper]]s.''[[Servants of the Empire: The Secret Academy]]'' His illegitimate son, [[General]] [[Armitage Hux]], incorporated his [[Parent|father's]] views into the [[First Order military|military]] training program of the [[First Order]],''[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens: The Visual Dictionary]]'' causing an entire generation to become [[First Order stormtrooper|stormtroopers]]—super [[soldier]]s{{Databank|first-order-stormtroopers|First Order Stormtroopers}} programmed from birth and exceptionally trained in Armitage's view. ==Behind the scenes== Jedi younglings were first introduced in the [[2002]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], the second installment of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[prequel trilogy]]. ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= *''[[The High Republic: Convergence]]'' {{Ab|The High Republic: Convergence
